resources.name: Suicide Prevention Hotline Ethiopia
resources.notes: National mental health crisis line operated through St. Paul's Hospital Millennium Medical College

resources.name: Agar Ethiopia
resources.notes: Psychosocial support and mental health services across Ethiopia

resources.name: Action Against Hunger Ethiopia
resources.notes: Mental health and psychosocial support in crisis-affected areas

resources.name: Access Now Digital Security Helpline
resources.notes: Free, confidential digital security support for activists and journalists

resources.name: Frontline Defenders
resources.notes: Emergency support for human rights defenders at risk

safety_plan.instruction: Identify one trusted person you can contact immediately - share your location with them.
Move to a safe, public, well-lit space if you feel physically at risk.
Contact the national crisis hotline at +251 116 629 714 if you are in mental health crisis.

resources.name: Sudan Mental Health Crisis Support - WHO Partnership

contact.availability: Office hours - conflict zones may have limited access

resources.notes: Coordinated through WHO Sudan office. Coverage may vary by region due to ongoing conflict.

resources.name: International Medical Corps Sudan
resources.notes: Mental health and psychosocial support programs across Sudan

resources.name: Medecins Sans Frontieres (MSF) Sudan
resources.notes: Psychosocial and mental health support in conflict-affected areas including Darfur and Khartoum

resources.name: Access Now Digital Security Helpline
resources.notes: Free, confidential digital security support. Use Signal for secure communication: +1 646 904 6871

resources.name: Frontline Defenders Emergency
resources.notes: Emergency support for Sudanese human rights defenders and journalists

resources.name: Committee to Protect Journalists - Emergency
resources.notes: Emergency assistance for journalists facing threats in Sudan

safety_plan.instruction: Identify a safe location in advance - know two exits from any location you work from.
Share your movement plan with a trusted contact outside Sudan if possible.
For digital threats, contact Access Now immediately using Signal: +1 646 904 6871.
For physical threats as a journalist or activist, contact Frontline Defenders emergency line.

resources.name: Somali Mental Health Foundation
resources.notes: Primary mental health NGO in Somalia. Coverage strongest in Mogadishu and Hargeisa.

resources.name: Medecins Sans Frontieres (MSF) Somalia
resources.notes: Mental health and psychosocial support in Mogadishu and other locations

resources.name: International Committee of the Red Cross - Somalia
resources.notes: Psychosocial support and protection for conflict-affected populations

resources.name: Access Now Digital Security Helpline
resources.notes: Free, confidential digital security support for Somali journalists and activists. Supports low-bandwidth connection.

resources.name: Frontline Defenders Emergency
resources.notes: Emergency support for Somali human rights defenders

safety_plan.instruction: Establish a daily check-in protocol with a trusted contact - agree on a signal if you cannot respond.
Keep emergency contacts saved offline on your device, not just in cloud-based apps.
For digital security threats, reach out to Access Now Helpline - they support low-connectivity users.
Avoid sharing your location digitally unless using end-to-end encrypted apps.

resources.name: Centre de Sante Mentale de Bamako
resources.notes: Primary public mental health facility in Mali. Located in Bamako.

resources.name: Medecins Sans Frontieres (MSF) Mali
resources.notes: Mental health and psychosocial support in conflict-affected regions including Mopti and Segou

resources.name: International Rescue Committee - Mali
resources.notes: Psychosocial support and protection services for displaced populations

resources.name: Access Now Digital Security Helpline
resources.notes: Free, confidential digital security support for Malian activists and journalists. French language support available.

resources.name: Frontline Defenders Emergency
resources.notes: Emergency support for human rights defenders in Mali

resources.name: Reporters Without Borders (RSF) - Africa Desk
resources.notes: Support and advocacy for Malian journalists facing threats

safety_plan.instruction: Identify a trusted contact outside Mali who knows your working schedule.
Keep a list of emergency contacts on paper, not only on your phone.
If you work in northern or central Mali, always travel with a contingency plan known to your team.
For digital threats, contact Access Now Helpline - French language support is available.
